html:
An Egg is a Food Item obtained by feeding an "Imprisoned" Bird any type of Meat, Fish or meat food item (except raw Monster Meat, with the exception of Don't Starve Together). Eggs can also be found at Pengull nesting areas. In the Shipwrecked DLC, Eggs can also be occasionally found when chopping down Jungle Trees. In the Hamlet DLC, Eggs can also be occasionally found when chopping down Rainforest Trees.

Eggs can be used in Crock Pot recipes such as Bacon and Eggs, Pierogi, and Waffles. Like Meats, Eggs can be "given" to the Pig King in exchange for 1 Gold Nugget each. They can also be given to Pigs in order to befriend them as allies (or followers). Two Eggs are required to craft Birds of the World, one of Wickerbottom's Books. Players carrying or picking up Eggs will anger nearby Bunnymen, as they count as a meat.

Unlike Tallbird Eggs, Eggs cannot be hatched. When spoiled, an Egg will turn into a Rotten Egg, rather than Rot.


Cooked Egg

A Cooked Egg is the result of cooking an egg over Fire. In most cases, this will take place at a Campfire or Fire Pit as a result of the player "Cooking" the Egg (a Star Caller's Staff can also be used). In some cases, an Egg may be cooked due to a Mob being set on fire.

Cooked Eggs can also be used in Crock Pot recipes, or given to Pigs or the Pig King. When a Cooked Egg spoils it turns into rot, not a rotten egg as it once did.

Harvesting

Cooked varieties of the following Foods can also be used. Any raw Food items below are also allowed when cooked, but raw versions of cooked items below are not allowed.

Tips

  • Meats that are not accepted by the Pig King can be converted into Eggs by the bird in a Birdcage, which are accepted in exchange for Gold Nuggets.
  • Spoiling Meats can be saved from turning into Rot by converting them into fresh Eggs.
  • Crock Pot recipes using Eggs can replace many recipes that use meats if the player has a Birdcage.
  • Giving two cooked Monster Meat to a Caged Bird will give two Eggs. These Eggs, along with one Morsel and one more Monster Meat will result in Bacon and Eggs. (75 Hunger and 20 Health from 3 Monster Meat and 1 Morsel).
  • Unlike other meats, raw eggs will not lower sanity when eaten.

Trivia

  • When Eggs/Cooked Eggs started to spoil, players used to be able to give them to a Birdcage to make them fresh again. However, one could only give the bird cooked eggs in order to receive another egg. This can no longer be done in Don't Starve Together after the Reap What You Sow Update, and cannot be done in the base game without switching to the pre2023update branch.
  • Wanda's examination quote for the raw egg is a reference to William Shakespeare's Tragedy Play "Macbeth". The quote is taken directly from the Play's script.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“A small, normal egg.”
–Wilson, when examining an Egg.
“It smells like a bird's butt.”
–Willow, when examining an Egg.
“Poor birdy.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an Egg.
“So much hope, dashed.”
–Wendy, when examining an Egg.
“I HATE YOU, EGG”
–WX-78, when examining an Egg.
“It is unfertilized.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an Egg.
“There's a bird in there thinking evil bird thoughts.”
–Woodie, when examining an Egg.
“I like to think of it as a baby bird prison.”
–Maxwell, when examining an Egg.
“Raw egg will unquestionably cause indigestion.”
–Wagstaff, when examining an Egg.
“Eggy.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an Egg.
“A hard shelled egg.”
–Webber, when examining an Egg.
“There is a tiny birdy buddy in there!”
–Walani, when examining an Egg.
“Nature perfects food.”
–Warly, when examining an Egg.
“A cackler's egg.”
–Woodlegs, when examining an Egg.
“IS'T FOOD'ST FOR WILBA BELLY.”
–Wilba, when examining an Egg.
“Baby Tweeter”
–Wormwood, when examining an Egg.
“Perfectly projectile shaped.”
–Wheeler, when examining an Egg.
“Breakfast.”
–Winona, when examining an Egg.
“An egg from a bird, or so I've heard.”
–Wortox, when examining an Egg.
“Baby bird inside?”
–Wurt, when examining an Egg.
“Egg-cellent! Get it? Because... egg...?”
–Walter, when examining an Egg.
“What, you egg?”
–Wanda, when examining an Egg.
An Egg is a Food Item obtained by feeding an "Imprisoned" Bird any type of Meat, Fish or meat food item (except raw Monster Meat, with the exception of Don't Starve Together). Eggs can also be found at Pengull nesting areas. In the Shipwrecked DLC, Eggs can also be occasionally found when chopping down Jungle Trees. In the Hamlet DLC, Eggs can also be occasionally found when chopping down Rainforest Trees.
Eggs can be used in Crock Pot recipes such as Bacon and Eggs, Pierogi, and Waffles. Like Meats, Eggs can be "given" to the Pig King in exchange for 1 Gold Nugget each. They can also be given to Pigs in order to befriend them as allies (or followers). Two Eggs are required to craft Birds of the World, one of Wickerbottom's Books. Players carrying or picking up Eggs will anger nearby Bunnymen, as they count as a meat.
Unlike Tallbird Eggs, Eggs cannot be hatched. When spoiled, an Egg will turn into a Rotten Egg, rather than Rot.
Cooked Egg[edit | edit source]
“Sunny side yum!”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Yuck. The yellow part is all runny.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Yum!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“No bird will ever come of it.”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“THIS IS SUPPOSED TO BE AN IMPROVEMENT”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“If only I had some toast.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Well, at least it never became a bird.”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Sunny side up.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Mmm mmm! Palatable protein.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Höt egg.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Fried just like mum did.”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“...I'm sorry, birdy buddy.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Could use a few different herbs...”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“If only I had me a crust o'bread.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“'TIS FWOOSHED EATS.”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Goes in belly”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Drat. I was trying to make scrambled eggs.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“I always get bits of shell in there by accident.”
–Winona, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Probably a mortal thing.”
–Wortox, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Don't want it!”
–Wurt, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Smells like breakfast is ready!”
–Walter, when examining a Cooked Egg.
“Whenever I make eggs they end up either undercooked or burnt.”
–Wanda, when examining a Cooked Egg.
A Cooked Egg is the result of cooking an egg over Fire. In most cases, this will take place at a Campfire or Fire Pit as a result of the player "Cooking" the Egg (a Star Caller's Staff can also be used). In some cases, an Egg may be cooked due to a Mob being set on fire.
Cooked Eggs can also be used in Crock Pot recipes, or given to Pigs or the Pig King. When a Cooked Egg spoils it turns into rot, not a rotten egg as it once did.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Trứng là Vật phẩm thu được bằng cách cho Chim "bị giam cầm" ăn bất kỳ loại Thịt, Cá hoặc thực phẩm thịt nào (ngoại trừ Thịt Quái sống, ngoại trừ Don't Starve Together). Trứng cũng có thể được tìm thấy ở khu vực làm tổ của Cánh Cụt Biển. Trong Shipwrecked DLC, Trứng đôi khi cũng có thể được tìm thấy khi chặt Cây Rừng. Trong Hamlet DLC, Trứng đôi khi cũng có thể được tìm thấy khi chặt Cây Rừng Nhiệt Đới.
